statement: The standard genetic code sits in the top 0.6% of random colorings of Q_6 under a block-preserving (Freeland-Hurst) null for physicochemical edge-mismatch
Organisms with codon-graph disconnections show elevated tRNA gene copies for the reassigned amino acid, compared to phylogenetically appropriate controls (4/4 cases)
Codon reassignment bit-flip distribution is non-uniform across the 6 GF(2)^6 coordinates at p<0.05 even under realistic Ts/Tv-weighted nulls (position-sensitive)

Systematic survey of Hamming-graph connectivity across 25 NCBI translation tables identifies 4 variant-code cases with amino-acid disconnection at ε=1 (Thr in yeast mito, Leu in chlorophycean mito, Ala in Pachysolen, Ser tripartite in Candida)
XOR 'Fano' relationships in GF(2)^6 predict enrichment of specific amino acids at KRAS G12 co-mutation sites
Serine's minimum UCN-AGY Hamming distance = 4 holds invariantly across all 24 base-to-bit encodings
PSL(2,7) is the fundamental symmetry group of the genetic code
The coordinate-wise map GF(2)^6 -> C^3 sending base-pairs to fourth roots of unity is a holomorphic embedding extending a character of GF(8)*
All 2-fold synonymous codon pairs in the standard code differ at exactly bit position 5 under the default encoding
All 4-fold synonymous codon groups share a 4-bit prefix with 2-bit suffixes exhausting GF(2)^2
